name: Circuit of the Abesse and Ardy Ponds
description: Hike the moderate 10.7-mile Saint-Paul-Lés-Dax and Abesse pond loop, exploring diverse landscapes and historical sites in 4 hours and 25 min
region_intro: Hiking around Mées offers a diverse landscape characterized by its unique geological formations and varied terrain. The region features a mix of flat, forested areas and gentle elevations, providing accessible paths for different skill levels. Trails often traverse through natural areas, showcasing local flora and tranquil environments. The area's topography is generally mild, making it suitable for leisurely walks and moderate hikes. Best hiking trails around Mées The most popular hiking route is Saint-Paul-Lés-Dax and the Abesse pond - loop, a 10.7 miles (17.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of forest paths and open water views. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Estanque Peat Bog loop from Angoumé, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail winds through natural peat bog landscapes, offering a unique ecological experience. Local hikers also love the The Estanque Peat Bog loop from Mées, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through the peat bog area, often completed in about 44 minutes. Hiking around Mées is defined by tranquil forest paths, open landscapes, and gentle elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Mées are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Mées's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What makes hiking in Mées unique?
A: Mées is renowned for its extraordinary geological formations, particularly the &lt;strong&gt;Pénitents des Mées&lt;/strong&gt;. These impressive rock pillars, some reaching up to 100 meters, stretch for over two kilometers and offer a dramatic backdrop to many trails. The region also features extensive olive groves and panoramic views of the Durance valley and Montagne de Lure.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in Mées?
A: Yes, Mées offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities. There are over 100 routes available, catering to various preferences. You'll find a good mix of easy strolls and moderate excursions.

Q: What are the best trails for experiencing the Pénitents des Mées?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Sentier des Pénitents&lt;/strong&gt; is a dedicated trail offering spectacular views of these rock formations. It's a moderate 4.34 km loop that takes about 1 hour and 32 minutes. An easier version, 'Chapelle Saint-Roch by the sentier des Pénitents,' is also available. Another option is the &lt;strong&gt;Sentier de San Peyre&lt;/strong&gt;, which follows a portion of the Pénitents trail and leads to a belvedere with expansive views.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Mées?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;strong&gt;Chemin des Oliviers&lt;/strong&gt; is an easy, family-friendly loop, approximately 3.7 km long, taking about 1 hour and 30 minutes. It allows hikers to discover the region's ancient olive groves. Many of the 76 easy routes available in Mées are suitable for families.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Mées?
A: Hikes in Mées offer diverse views. You can expect stunning close-ups of the &lt;strong&gt;Pénitents des Mées&lt;/strong&gt;, panoramic vistas over the Durance valley and the Montagne de Lure, and serene landscapes of ancient olive groves. Trails like the &lt;strong&gt;Chemin de la Haute-Montagne&lt;/strong&gt; also provide magnificent open views over the Haute-Montagne plateau.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in Mées?
A: Along the trails, you can visit the &lt;strong&gt;Chapelle Saint-Roch&lt;/strong&gt;, which offers remarkable panoramas over the Durance valley. The region itself is a natural gateway to the UNESCO Geopark of Haute-Provence. For more local points of interest, you might explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2918452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Hot Fountain&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5962104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maison de la Barthe&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Mées?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Mées are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;strong&gt;Sentier des Pénitents&lt;/strong&gt; is a loop, and the &lt;strong&gt;Chemin des Oliviers&lt;/strong&gt; is also a family-friendly loop. You'll find numerous options for circular walks among the available routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mées?
A: The hiking routes in Mées are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique geological formations, the peaceful olive groves, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Is Mées a good destination for nature lovers?
A: Absolutely. Mées is rich in natural beauty, from the striking &lt;strong&gt;Pénitents des Mées&lt;/strong&gt; to the extensive olive groves and the Mediterranean flora found within the Sensitive Natural Area. The diverse landscapes and geological history make it a fascinating place for nature enthusiasts.

Q: Where can I find more information about the olive groves in Mées?
A: Mées is the leading olive-growing commune in the Alpes-de-Haute-Provence department. You can explore this heritage directly on the &lt;strong&gt;Chemin des Oliviers&lt;/strong&gt; trail. For additional information on the region's olive path, you may find details on the local tourism website: &lt;a href="https://en.tourisme-alpes-haute-provence.com/practice-activities/788727_pedestrian-sports-les-mees-olive-path/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedestrian Sports - Les Mées Olive Path&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mées?
A: While specific seasonal weather information isn't provided, the Mediterranean climate of the Alpes-de-Haute-Provence generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are often preferred, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions.
tips: Admire the flora and fauna of this pond. A quiet place to take a break and relax.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the "Saint-Paul-Lés-Dax and the Abesse pond - loop" trail is not detailed in the available resources. However, as the trail is known as the "Circuit des Étangs d'Abesse et d'Ardy" and is located in Saint-Paul-lès-Dax, it's generally advisable to look for parking near the Abesse pond area or in Saint-Paul-lès-Dax itself, which serves as a common starting point for local trails.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The available information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or if there are specific leash rules for the "Saint-Paul-Lés-Dax and the Abesse pond - loop" trail. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets before you go.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse landscapes including plains, forests, and bodies of water. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery for hiking in this region. Summer can be warm, while winter might be cooler but still suitable depending on weather conditions. Consider visiting when the foliage is vibrant or when the weather is mild for the most enjoyable experience.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required?
faqs.answer: Based on the provided information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for hiking the "Saint-Paul-Lés-Dax and the Abesse pond - loop" trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the hike?
faqs.answer: For hikers, the loop trail is considered to have a medium difficulty and is estimated to take approximately 4 hours and 30 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 15.2 km (9.4 miles) circuit.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse landscape, taking you through plains, forests, and past several bodies of water, including the Abesse Pond, as well as lakes like Christus, Curve, and Poustagnacq. You'll experience green spaces, cross a bridge, and walk through a park, providing a varied connection to nature.

faqs.question: What are the notable points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the scenic Abesse Pond and other lakes, the route is rich in history. You'll pass through sites where old forges, Abesse and Ardy, once stood. The circuit also leads to Tinon, marked by a distinctive stone indicating the boundary of five communes. The area has a history tied to the metallurgical industry, and you'll follow parts of the former wooden railway, now the "chemin d'Abesse," which once transported iron.

faqs.question: Is the Saint-Paul-Lés-Dax and Abesse pond loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate difficulty for hiking. While not extremely challenging, its length of 17.2 km (approximately 10.7 miles) and estimated duration of 4 hours and 30 minutes might be a significant undertaking for absolute beginners. Hikers with some experience or a good level of fitness should find it manageable.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Circuits des Étangs, EV3 France 47, Circuits d'Abesse, and various Chemin Rural paths such as Chemin Rural de Bois, Chemin Rural d'Artigues, Chemin Rural de l'Union, Chemin Rural du Bayle, Chemin Rural de Tinon, and Chemin Rural de Bouché.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Saint-Paul-Lés-Dax and the Abesse pond - loop" is a circular trail, meaning you will end up back at your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Can I expect to see wildlife or unique flora?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses plains, forests, and wetlands around the ponds, creating habitats for various wildlife. While specific sightings cannot be guaranteed, you may encounter local bird species, small mammals, and diverse plant life typical of the Landes region. The rich green spaces and bodies of water contribute to a varied ecosystem.
